# Masemi

*A world generated by Cartogenesis v0.11.0 from seed `vahalia`. Regenerate it exactly with `node src/cli.ts generate --seed vahalia`.*

## Overview

- **Extent:** 360 × 360 cells
- **Land / ocean:** 55.2% land, 44.6% ocean, 8 lake(s)
- **Climate & life:** dominant biome **Savanna**, 16 biomes in all
- **Peoples:** 33 regions, 22 settlements, 3 realms
- **Capital:** Monadianale, seat of House Osiriano
- **Dominant power:** Vobica (of 2 surviving realm(s))
- **The present year:** 1174 After Reckoning

## Notable features

- **Mount Stobrabrau** — the highest peak
- **The Qaansauesh** — the greatest river
- **Lake Miti** — the largest inland water

- Khemazaa is a coast of golden savanna, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.
- Greirnbei is an inland reach of windswept scrub, its folk hardy and sea-wise.
- Deoriapauriabe is a coast of rolling grassland, a country of vineyards and old stone towns, seat of Loti.
- Bohragard is a coast of frozen barrens, its folk hardy and sea-wise, seat of Knastbornbau.
- Tenka is an inland reach of sun-scoured dunes, its people caravan-traders and readers of stars.

## Notable figures

- **Bonaavento, the Architect** — Bonaavento raised the great road between Monadianale and Peonimortia.
- **Naciapiaa, the Heretic** — Naciapiaa was exiled from Monadianale for denying the old gods.
- **Seossosenasiavento, the Scholar** — Seossosenasiavento mapped the waters of Miti.
- **Nitaulenpia, the Explorer** — Nitaulenpia charted the far coasts and traced the Qaansauesh to its source.
- **Badata, the General** — Badata broke the long siege of Loti.

## Faiths

### the Cult of Qanqa — Qanqa, god of the Sun

> Each dawn Qanqa drives the sun over Stobrabrau; the people of Khemazaa greet it with fire.

Followed across 10 region(s).

### the Rite of Fjaagard — Fjaagard, god of the Hearth

> Fjaagard lit the first hearth in Greirnbei, and its flame has never since gone out.

Followed across 9 region(s).

### the Faith of Raramorra — Raramorra, god of the Storm

> Raramorra rides the storms down the Qaansauesh, and Deoriapauriabe both fears and blesses the rain.

Followed across 9 region(s).

### the Rite of Eliiaaeeiel — Eliiaaeeiel, god of the Harvest

> Eliiaaeeiel scattered the first seed across Elaennimela; the golden fields were the gift, and the tithe the price.

Followed across 5 region(s).

## Resources & trade

- **Major exports:** Livestock, Horses, Grain, Timber, Fish
- **Wealthiest settlement:** Skorkbyogard
- **Trade hubs:** Loti, Skorkbyogard, Jasha
- **Deposits:** Iron ×26, Copper ×19, Gold ×8, Gems ×5, Stone ×51, Coal ×22, Timber ×12, Grain ×36, Livestock ×138, Fish ×14, Furs ×14, Spices ×2, Salt ×22, Horses ×48, Wine ×15

## Legends of the founding age

- **31 After Reckoning** — The city of Monadianale was founded amid good country, destined to become the seat of power.
- **38 After Reckoning** — The city of Peonimortia was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **60 After Reckoning** — Monadianale rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Masemi was proclaimed.
- **65 After Reckoning** — The city of Loti was founded on a sheltered harbour.
- **69 After Reckoning** — Peonimortia rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Vobica was proclaimed.
- **91 After Reckoning** — Loti rose to rule its hinterland, and the realm of Delenca was proclaimed.
- **545 After Reckoning** — Stobrabrau woke in fire and ash; a hard winter followed, and harvests failed across the land.
- **585 After Reckoning** — An academy founded on the shores of Miti gathered the age's finest cartographers.
- **940 After Reckoning** — A great storm-tide flooded Peonimortia; the lower quarters were lost to the sea.
- **1112 After Reckoning** — Under a long peace, Monadianale flourished; its libraries and roads became the wonder of the world.
